turns.content: What brings you in today?
My wife insisted I come in. It's been about three years since my last physical, and she wants me to get my blood pressure and cholesterol checked. I feel fine, honestly—no complaints.
When did this start?
Nothing really started. It's just been a while since my last checkup, so my wife wanted me to get everything looked at. No symptoms or anything like that.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?
I don't have any pain or discomfort. Everything feels normal to me.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No, none of that. I've been feeling fine—no fevers, no chills, nothing out of the ordinary.
What medications are you currently taking?
I'm not on any prescription medications right now. I take an occasional over-the-counter pain reliever if I overdo it at work, but that's about it.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
I don't have any chronic conditions I know of. My doctor mentioned my blood pressure was a bit high a few years back, but I never really followed up on it. No diabetes or anything like that.
Are you allergic to any medications?
No, I'm not allergic to any medications that I'm aware of. Never had a reaction to anything.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?
I haven't tried anything since there's nothing to relieve. I'm not in pain or feeling sick—just here for the routine checkup my wife wanted.
Does anything make it better or worse?
No, nothing really makes it better or worse because there's nothing to change. I feel the same day to day.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's not affecting my daily life at all. I'm working full-time as a construction manager, staying active on the job site, sleeping fine, eating okay. Just here for a checkup—nothing's slowing me down.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
I don't smoke regularly, but I'll have a cigarette now and then if I'm out with the guys. As for drinking, I'll have a couple of beers most evenings after work—nothing heavy, just to wind down.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?
My father had high blood pressure, and my mother was diabetic. Nothing out of the ordinary for folks their age, I suppose.
Have you experienced anything like this before?
No, I haven't experienced anything like this before because there's really nothing to experience. Just routine blood work and a physical—same as any other checkup I've had in the past.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?
Physically, I'd say a 1 — no pain or discomfort at all. Emotionally, also a 1 — I'm not stressed or worried, just here to get the checkup done.
Is there anything else you think I should know?
No, I think that about covers it. Like I said, I feel fine overall. If my wife's got any specific concerns she hasn't mentioned to me, she'll probably bring them up herself.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
Honestly, there's nothing to cope with. I'm not worried about it, just getting it over with so my wife can stop nagging me.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
I already told you — there's nothing to cope with. I'm fine, no stress, no symptoms. Just here for the routine stuff.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
I appreciate you asking again, but I've been telling you — there's nothing to cope with. No pain, no stress, no issues. Just a routine physical. That's all.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

Look, I get that you're being thorough, but I've answered this the same way every time: I'm not coping with anything because there's nothing wrong. No symptoms, no stress. Let's just move on to the exam if that's okay.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I'm starting to feel a little frustrated here, honestly. I've told you three times now — I'm not coping with anything because there's nothing going on. No pain, no distress, no worry. Can we please just proceed with the physical?
